message:
Add primary-root-only preflight semantics and repo_updater retry logic

Dependency preflight now distinguishes primary vs non-primary dependency
roots. Previously, any Missing/Stale/Unknown dependency root would block
remote execution entirely. This caused false-positive blocking when
sibling repos had intermittent verification issues but were already
cached on the worker. Now:

- Only primary roots (is_primary=true) can block remote execution.
- Non-primary dependency roots with issues are logged as warnings but
  do not abort the build. Cargo will surface clear errors if a path
  dependency is truly unavailable on the worker.
- The fail-open path in execute_remote_compilation no longer returns
  an error. Instead it proceeds with primary-root-only sync, which is
  better than falling back to local execution (which defeats rch's
  purpose).

Added is_primary field to DependencyPreflightEvidence struct and wired
it through build_dependency_preflight_report and the fail-open report
builder.

Also adds exponential-backoff retry logic to execute_repo_updater_command.
Previously, a single transient SSH failure would immediately fall back
to direct sync. Now the function respects the contract's retry_policy
(max_attempts, initial_backoff_ms, backoff_multiplier_percent,
max_backoff_ms), retrying with exponential backoff before giving up.

Updated existing tests to set is_primary=true where blocking behavior
is expected, and added new tests:
- test_non_primary_missing_deps_do_not_block_preflight
- test_non_primary_stale_deps_do_not_block_preflight
- Disabled mock override in stale verification test for proper coverage

Formatting improvements to multi-line assert macros and method chains
throughout the test module.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
